plan.problem: One-way OSPF Hello reception on R2 Serial0/1/0 causing adjacency to remain down and preventing route exchange between R1 and R2.
plan.fix_summary: On router2, configure Serial0/1/0 with the expected IP (10.0.0.2/30), ensure the interface is administratively up, and enable OSPF on the interface (OSPF process 1, area 0) so Hellos are transmitted/received and the adjacency can form.

messages.content:
What was observed
- One‑way OSPF on R1<->R2 Serial0/1/0: R1 showed R2 in INIT, R2 reported no neighbor despite the interface being up.

Diagnosis (root cause)
- An inbound ACL named R2_IN on R2 Serial0/1/0 was denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5/224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving R1’s Hellos.

Changes made
- Ensured Serial0/1/0 on router2 configured with 10.0.0.2/30.
- Deleted the ACL object R2_IN from router2 (attempt to detach found no ACL bound inbound).

Verification (passed/failed and remaining)
- Passed: R2 now shows adjacency to 1.1.1.1 on Serial0/1/0 (ospf-nbr-full) and the interface is up — adjacency and route exchange restored.
- Remaining: Running config still contains an ip access-group in on Serial0/1/0 referencing "R2_IN" while the ACL definition no longer exists — configuration inconsistency. Recommendation: remove the access-group from the interface or recreate/replace the intended ACL to match policy.
